##NAMES=Zincochenite
##RRUFFID=R250060
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Pb_4_Zn(OH)_6_(SO_4_)_2_
##LOCALITY=Redmond mine, North Carolina, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Tightly intergrown subparallel clusters of light blue crystals vary in habit including tablets, blades, and prisms in association with bechererite, chenite, cherokeeite, elyite, lanarkite, and steverustite. Type sample, described in Kampf et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250060
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Pb_4.00_(Zn_0.94_Cu^2+^_0.06_)(SO_4_)_1.81_(S_2_O_3_)_0.19_(OH)_6_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.89 b: 7.942 c: 7.958 alpha: 110.674 beta: 98.473 gamma: 100.019 volume: 334.06 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
